spec_match.rationale: The page now pairs the narrative with a live StorageManager.estimate() call, exposing the quota, usage, available bytes, old <500 MB detector result, and 10 GiB floor comparison.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified that the live estimate panel auto-runs on page load and the Run live estimate button calls navigator.storage.estimate() again, updating the timestamp and readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: The live panel handles unsupported StorageManager.estimate(), rejected promises, old detector thresholds, and the current-browser case where Chrome 148 returns 5.0 GiB, below the Chrome 151 target floor but above the old Incognito detector threshold.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section as the design system requires.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap at /v151/incognitostaticstoragequota/privacy-leak-explainer/?verify=before-live-estimate: navigator.storage.estimate was available, but the concept page had zero script calls, no buttons, and no live readout IDs. The repaired page was tested at /v151/incognitostaticstoragequota/privacy-leak-explainer/?verify=after-live-estimate-final: the live panel auto-ran and the Run live estimate button reran navigator.storage.estimate(), reporting quota 5.0 GiB (5,368,709,120 bytes), usage 0 B, available 5.0 GiB, an old <500 MB detector result of not flagged, and a Chrome 151 floor warning for the current Chrome 148 context. The page and conformance route had no console messages or failed requests; the existing /conformance/ route reported 5 PASS / 1 FAIL, with only the v151 10 GiB floor assertion failing in Chrome 148.
